strands.title: System Prompt Waste Analysis Project Scope & Phase Transition
strands.content: A system prompt analyzer module (src/pichay/analyzer.py) is being planned to measure waste and inefficiency in API calls across logged sessions. The core premise: system prompts are often reused verbatim, contain unused components, and have duplicated content—all representing wasted context tokens. The project focuses on instrumenting existing proxy JSONL logs to decompose prompts and measure waste quantitatively. This integrates into a larger pichay system already containing proxy.py (log capture) and eval.py (evaluation framework). The analyzer should produce structured results with dataclasses and expose functionality via CLI. **CYCLE 5 INTEGRATION: PRIORITY 1 task execution has begun. The [tool_result: Read] block has been received with the opening of proxy.py source code. This represents the transition from 'awaiting code content' to 'in receipt of code content and actively extracting findings.' The file header indicates proxy.py is a logging proxy for Claude API calls with two operational modes: Observe (default, logs request/response metrics, pure output) and presumably a second mode. This opening segment reveals: (1) proxy.py is an executable CLI tool (#!/usr/bin/env python3), (2) it focuses on metrics logging and request/response capture, (3) operational modes affect what/how data is logged. The tensor must now track findings as they emerge from the code content and map them against PRIORITY 1 success criteria. Cycle 5 is the first code-examination cycle; incomplete code reception is flagged as a declared loss.**

key_claims.text: System prompts in API logs contain multiple distinct semantic components (tool defs, skill defs, CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, git status, IDE context, conversation instructions) that can be pattern-matched and decomposed
Most tool definitions are static across turns, while components like git status are dynamic; re-sending identical static components wastes tokens
A significant portion of tool definitions in system prompts are never invoked in actual tool_use blocks in messages
Skills and other semantic components appear multiple times (often tripled) within a single system prompt, representing duplicate waste
The project is now in operational execution phase with externally-persistent todo list; todos have been successfully created and are ready to be advanced
PRIORITY 1 task (read/analyze proxy.py and eval.py) has entered active execution phase in cycle 4 and is receiving code content in cycle 5

strands.title: Functional Requirements & Decomposition Strategy—Now Task-Backed
strands.content: The analyzer must implement five core analyses: (1) COMPONENT DECOMPOSITION—parse system prompt text and segment it by semantic block using pattern matching on text boundaries. (2) STATIC vs DYNAMIC ANALYSIS—track whether each component changes across turns within a session; measure bytes of static content re-sent unnecessarily. (3) TOOL USAGE TRACKING—extract tool definitions from system prompt, cross-reference against actual tool_use blocks in message records, identify defined-but-unused tools, and sum their byte cost. (4) DUPLICATE DETECTION—find identical or near-identical semantic blocks appearing multiple times in a single prompt; report byte counts. (5) PER-SESSION SUMMARY—aggregate metrics as absolute bytes and percentages. The module must use dataclasses to structure results, expose a CLI entry point, and require careful study of proxy.py and eval.py before implementation. **CYCLE 5 INTEGRATION: Code content from proxy.py has arrived. Initial examination reveals: (a) proxy.py is an executable CLI script (line 1: #!/usr/bin/env python3), (b) it performs 'logging proxy' functionality for Claude API calls (line 2 docstring), (c) it has two operational modes: 'Observe' (default) and at least one other (lines 4-5), (d) 'Observe' mode logs request/response metrics and generates pure output. This opening suggests proxy.py is responsible for capturing and logging API interactions in a structured format. The analyzer must eventually understand: how the log format is structured, where system_prompt_full is stored, how tool_use blocks are represented. Further code examination is needed to extract JSONL schema details. The findings artifact should begin tracking: 'proxy.py is CLI tool with Observe mode logging request/response metrics to persistent format (format/location TBD)'.**

key_claims.text: Component decomposition must rely on pattern matching over heuristic text segmentation to identify tool definitions, skill blocks, and metadata sections
Static vs dynamic analysis requires tracking component state across multiple turns within a session to measure re-transmission waste
Tool usage tracking depends on correctly parsing tool_use blocks in message JSON and matching their names against tool definitions in the system prompt
Duplicate detection should quantify byte cost of repeated semantic blocks, likely to reveal skills being tripled or other structural redundancy
Per-session summary metrics should report both absolute byte counts and percentages to allow comparative analysis across sessions and cost models
proxy.py is a CLI logging tool that captures Claude API calls and stores them in a persistent log format, likely JSONL

strands.title: Code Examination Phase 1: proxy.py Structure Discovery
strands.content: Code content from proxy.py has been received (lines 1-5, partial). The file opens with a Python 3 executable shebang and docstring describing a 'logging proxy for Claude API calls, with optional context paging.' Two operational modes are documented: (1) **Observe** (default)—logs request/response metrics, produces pure output (no modification/paging of context). This suggests that in Observe mode, proxy.py acts as a pure logging/observation tool that records API interactions without altering them. A second mode is mentioned but not named in the received content. The docstring structure ('Two modes:' followed by 'Observe (default):...') suggests the file will document both modes clearly. **CYCLE 5 DISCOVERY**: This opening provides several key findings for PRIORITY 1: (a) proxy.py is designed as an executable CLI script, (b) it operates as a 'logging proxy'—meaning it intercepts/observes Claude API calls and logs them, (c) Observe mode captures request/response metrics without altering context, (d) there is at least one alternative mode (not yet named in received content). To complete PRIORITY 1, future cycles must receive more of proxy.py to understand: (e) the exact log format (JSONL?), (f) log file location/naming, (g) where system_prompt_full is stored in request records, (h) the structure of message and tool_use data in response records, (i) the second operational mode and its differences from Observe. The received content is incomplete and represents a partial finding checkpoint rather than comprehensive discovery.

key_claims.text: proxy.py is a CLI-based logging proxy that captures Claude API calls for observation and analysis
proxy.py has at least two operational modes: Observe (default, logging-only) and one other (unnamed in received content)
In Observe mode, proxy.py logs request/response metrics without modifying or paging the context data
proxy.py is designed as an executable Python 3 script, suitable for CLI invocation from the command line
The received code content (lines 1-5) is incomplete and represents only the file header and opening docstring

strands.title: Execution Phase & Todo List Coordination—Cycle 5 In-Progress Status
strands.content: PRIORITY 1 task is actively in-progress. Code content from proxy.py has been received and is being examined in real-time. The tensor projector is transitioning from 'awaiting code content' to 'receiving and analyzing code content.' Cycle 5 represents the first of what may be multiple code-examination cycles. The [tool_result: Read] indicates that file reading is occurring, and the partial content received (lines 1-5 out of an unknown total) suggests either: (1) the file is being streamed in chunks, (2) the user is providing a representative excerpt, or (3) the read was truncated at context boundaries. The tensor must: (a) track that PRIORITY 1 is now in 'code examination' sub-phase, (b) document all findings as they emerge, (c) flag incomplete code reception as a declared loss, (d) anticipate receiving more code in subsequent cycles, (e) maintain a 'findings checkpoint' that can survive cycle boundaries. **STATUS UPDATE**: PRIORITY 1 progress thus far: (1) proxy.py header/docstring received ✓, (2) operational modes (Observe + 1 other) identified ✓, (3) JSONL schema NOT YET EXTRACTED, (4) system_prompt_full location NOT YET FOUND, (5) tool_use block structure NOT YET FOUND, (6) eval.py NOT YET EXAMINED. The task is on-track but incomplete. Next cycle should either: (a) receive more proxy.py content, or (b) request explicit continuation of proxy.py reading, or (c) transition to eval.py examination if proxy.py delivery is complete. PRIORITY 2-3 (dataclass design, implementation scaffold) remain blocked pending full PRIORITY 1 completion.

key_claims.text: PRIORITY 1 task is actively in-progress in cycle 5; code content from proxy.py is being examined
The received proxy.py content (lines 1-5) is partial/incomplete and represents the file header and opening docstring only
At least two sub-components of PRIORITY 1 have progressed: (1) proxy.py header/docstring examined, (2) operational modes identified
Critical PRIORITY 1 findings remain pending: JSONL schema, system_prompt_full location, tool_use block structure, eval.py examination
PRIORITY 2-3 tasks are currently blocked pending full completion of PRIORITY 1 code examination and findings synthesis

The received content ends mid-sentence ('Pure o[') indicating either intentional truncation or tool cutoff. Future cycles will likely provide additional content, but current cycle lacks the complete file.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed JSONL record schema from proxy.py source; exact field names, data types, nesting, and required/optional fields
declared_losses.why: PRIORITY 1 requires understanding the log format to design analyzer.py. The file header provides context (logging proxy) but not schema details. More code content is needed to find the record creation, serialization, or documentation.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Location and structure of system_prompt_full field in request records; encoding, byte length tracking, potential truncation
declared_losses.why: Critical for analyzer.py to know where to find system prompts in logs. The header doesn't reveal this. Must examine actual record construction or serialization code in proxy.py.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Tool_use block representation in message JSON; exact structure, field names, how tools are invoked/named
declared_losses.why: Analyzer.py must cross-reference tool definitions against tool_use blocks. Log format details are not visible from file header alone. Requires examining message serialization or documentation in code.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Complete examination of eval.py; dataclass patterns, CLI design, code style conventions
declared_losses.why: PRIORITY 1 requires studying eval.py to establish code style and result patterns. Not yet examined. Must be explicitly requested in future cycles.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The name and description of the second operational mode in proxy.py (lines 6+ not received)
declared_losses.why: Docstring indicates 'Two modes:' but only Observe is described in received content. Understanding both modes is important context for how analyzer.py fits into the system.
